text:
Australian B-girl Rachael 'Raygun' Gunn has received public support from a popular comedian, who lauded her Olympics performance as "pretty courageous".

Viral Australian breakdancer Rachael 'Raygun' Gunn has received support from an unlikely source, with US comedian Arj Barker standing up for her outing at the Paris Olympics.

Raygun's performance at the 2024 Olympic Games turned heads globally, as her quirky form of breakdancing - which included bouncing around the stage like a kangaroo and doing the sprinkler - left viewers divided.

Gunn, who has a PhD in breakdancing and dance culture, was defeated in each of her three pool matches in the first women's Olympic breakdancing competition.

In all of her matches, her opponent was favoured by each of the nine judges in the scoring system.

The 36-year-old's dance moves drew heavy criticism and online trolling, with the Australian Olympic Team rallying to support her during the "devastating" backlash she received.

During a recent stand-up gig, Barker, 50, defended Gunn and the originality of her performance, arguing her uniqueness means she will always be remembered.

“I saw some memes that were pretty funny, and, I mean, it was pretty surprising what she did, but I don’t get all the hate, you know, like, I don’t get like, there’s a lot of people that are like 'you’re a disgrace' and, 'you’re a waste of taxpayers money,'” he told the audience at Melbourne's The Comic Lounge.

“For me, I don’t understand that, because I think at the very least, what she did was pretty courageous. You could say what you want about her, but I mean, she knew she wasn’t gonna win.

“She f***ing went out in style, man. And you know that other breakdancer, the one she was competing against? No, you don’t,” he added before the crowd laughed and applauded in response.

Gunn broke her silence on her drama-filled week on Thursday night AEST, releasing a video on her Instagram from Europe, where she is currently understood to be on holiday with her parents and husband Samuel Free.

“I didn’t realise that that would also open the door to so much hate, which has frankly been pretty devastating," the university lecturer said in her video.

"I worked my butt off preparing for the Olympics and I gave my all, truly.

“I am honoured to have been part of the Australian Olympic team and to be a part of breaking’s Olympic debut.”

The B-girl also condemned allegations over how she came to be selected for Australia's Olympic breaking team after theories emerged online via a petition on Change.org which has since been taken down.

The Australian Olympic Committee hit out at the allegations on Thursday, releasing a lengthy statement that outlined the "facts" of how Gunn secured qualification to the Games in Paris.

title: Comedian Arj Barker stands up for Australian B-girl Raygun amid relentless criticism over Paris Olympics performance | Sky News Australia
description: Viral Australian breakdancer Rachael 'Raygun' Gunn has received support from an unlikely source, with US comedian Arj Barker standing up for her outing at the Paris Olympics.
